title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description:
Given an array `heights` of bar heights (each of width 1), return the area of
the largest rectangle that fits entirely within the histogram.

solutions.summary: Keep an increasing stack of bar indices; when a shorter bar appears, settle the taller ones.

steps.title: Settle bars as a shorter one arrives
steps.explanation: A virtual zero-height bar at the end flushes the stack; each popped bar spans from the new index back to the previous stack entry.

code.python:
def largest_rectangle_area(heights):
    stack = []
    max_area = 0
    for i in range(len(heights) + 1):
        h = 0 if i == len(heights) else heights[i]
        while stack and heights[stack[-1]] >= h:
            height = heights[stack.pop()]
            width = i if not stack else i - stack[-1] - 1
            max_area = max(max_area, height * width)
        stack.append(i)
    return max_area
